contentBlocks.original:
स्तोत्रम् ।
नमस्तेऽस्तु भैरवाय ब्रह्मविष्णुशिवात्मने ।
नमस्त्रैलोक्यवन्द्याय वरदाय परात्मने ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
stōtram ।
namastē'stu bhairavāya brahmaviṣṇuśivātmanē ।
namastrailōkyavandyāya varadāya parātmanē ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to Bhairava, whose Self is Brahmā, Viṣṇu, and Śiva; salutation to the one worshipped by all three worlds, the boon-granter, the Supreme Self.
contentBlocks.meanings: namaste astu bhairavāya — salutation to Bhairava; brahma-viṣṇu-śiva-ātmanē — whose Self is Brahmā, Viṣṇu, and Śiva; namaḥ trailōkya-vandyāya — salutation to the one worshipped by the three worlds; varadāya — the boon-granter; parātmanē — the Supreme Self.

contentBlocks.original:
रत्नसिंहासनस्थाय दिव्याभरणशोभिने ।
दिव्यमाल्यविभूषाय नमस्ते दिव्यमूर्तये ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ratnasiṃhāsanasthāya divyābharaṇaśōbhinē ।
divyamālyavibhūṣāya namastē divyamūrtayē ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the one seated on a jeweled throne, resplendent with divine ornaments, adorned with divine garlands — salutation to the divine form.
contentBlocks.meanings: ratna-siṃhāsana-sthāya — seated on a jeweled lion-throne; divya-ābharaṇa-śōbhinē — resplendent with divine ornaments; divya-mālyavibhūṣāya — adorned with divine garlands; namastē divya-mūrtayē — salutation to the divine form.

contentBlocks.original:
नमस्तेऽनेकहस्ताय ह्यनेकशिरसे नमः ।
नमस्तेऽनेकनेत्राय ह्यनेकविभवे नमः ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namastē'nēkahastāya hyanēkaśirasē namaḥ ।
namastē'nēkanētrāya hyanēkavibhavē namaḥ ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the many-handed; salutation indeed to the many-headed; salutation to the many-eyed; salutation to the one of many glories.
contentBlocks.meanings: namastē anēka-hastāya — salutation to the one with many hands; hi anēka-śirasē namaḥ — salutation indeed to the many-headed; namastē anēka-nētrāya — salutation to the many-eyed; hi anēka-vibhavē namaḥ — salutation to the one of many glories/opulences.

contentBlocks.original:
नमस्तेऽनेककण्ठाय ह्यनेकांशाय ते नमः ।
नमोस्त्वनेकैश्वर्याय ह्यनेकदिव्यतेजसे ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namastē'nēkakaṇṭhāya hyanēkāṃśāya tē namaḥ ।
namōstvanēkaiśvaryāya hyanēkadivyatējasē ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the many-throated; salutation to the one of many portions; salutation to the one of many lordships; salutation to the many divine radiances.
contentBlocks.meanings: namastē anēka-kaṇṭhāya — salutation to the many-throated; hi anēka-aṃśāya tē namaḥ — salutation to the one of many portions/aspects; namastu anēka-aiśvaryāya — salutation to the one of many lordships; hi anēka-divya-tējasē — to the one of many divine radiances.

contentBlocks.original:
अनेकायुधयुक्ताय ह्यनेकसुरसेविने ।
अनेकगुणयुक्ताय महादेवाय ते नमः ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
anēkāyudhayuktāya hyanēkasurasēvinē ।
anēkaguṇayuktāya mahādēvāya tē namaḥ ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: To the one endowed with many weapons, to the one served by many gods, to the one endowed with many qualities — salutation to Mahādeva.
contentBlocks.meanings: anēka-āyudha-yuktāya — endowed with many weapons; hi anēka-sura-sēvinē — served by many gods; anēka-guṇa-yuktāya — endowed with many qualities; mahādēvāya tē namaḥ — salutation to you, Mahādeva.

contentBlocks.original:
नमो दारिद्र्यकालाय महासम्पत्प्रदायिने ।
श्रीभैरवीप्रयुक्ताय त्रिलोकेशाय ते नमः ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namō dāridryakālāya mahāsampatpradāyinē ।
śrībhairavīprayuktāya trilōkēśāya tē namaḥ ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the destroyer of poverty, to the bestower of great wealth, to the one endowed with Śrī Bhairavī (his consort), to the Lord of the three worlds — salutation.
contentBlocks.meanings: namaḥ dāridrya-kālāya — salutation to the destroyer/death of poverty; mahā-sampat-pradāyinē — to the bestower of great wealth; śrī-bhairavī-prayuktāya — to the one empowered/accompanied by Śrī Bhairavī; tri-lōkēśāya tē namaḥ — salutation to you, Lord of the three worlds.

contentBlocks.original:
दिगम्बर नमस्तुभ्यं दिगीशाय नमो नमः ।
नमोऽस्तु दैत्यकालाय पापकालाय ते नमः ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
digambara namastubhyaṃ digīśāya namō namaḥ ।
namō'stu daityakālāya pāpakālāya tē namaḥ ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: O sky-clad one (digambara) — salutation; to the lord of directions — repeated salutation. Salutation to the destroyer of demons; to the destroyer of sins — salutation.
contentBlocks.meanings: digambara — O sky-clad one (wearing the sky as garment = naked ascetic); namastubhyaṃ — salutation to you; digīśāya — to the lord of directions; namaḥ namaḥ — salutation, salutation; namastu daitya-kālāya — salutation to the destroyer of demons; pāpa-kālāya tē namaḥ — salutation to you, destroyer of sins.

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वज्ञाय नमस्तुभ्यं नमस्ते दिव्यचक्षुषे ।
अजिताय नमस्तुभ्यं जितामित्राय ते नमः ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvajñāya namastubhyaṃ namastē divyachakṣuṣē ।
ajitāya namastubhyaṃ jitāmitrāya tē namaḥ ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the omniscient; salutation to the divine-eyed. Salutation to the unconquered; salutation to the conqueror of enemies.
contentBlocks.meanings: sarvajñāya namastubhyaṃ — salutation to the omniscient; namastē divya-chakṣuṣē — salutation to the one of divine eyes; ajitāya namastubhyaṃ — salutation to the unconquered one; jita-amitrāya tē namaḥ — salutation to the one who has conquered enemies.

contentBlocks.original:
नमस्ते रुद्रपुत्राय गणनाथाय ते नमः ।
नमस्ते वीरवीराय महावीराय ते नमः ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namastē rudraputrāya gaṇanāthāya tē namaḥ ।
namastē vīravīrāya mahāvīrāya tē namaḥ ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the son of Rudra; salutation to the lord of the gaṇas. Salutation to the hero of heroes; salutation to the great hero.
contentBlocks.meanings: namastē rudra-putrāya — salutation to the son of Rudra; gaṇa-nāthāya tē namaḥ — salutation to you, lord of the gaṇas; namastē vīra-vīrāya — salutation to the hero of heroes; mahā-vīrāya tē namaḥ — salutation to you, the great hero.

contentBlocks.original:
नमोऽस्त्वनन्तवीर्याय महाघोराय ते नमः ।
नमस्ते घोरघोराय विश्वघोराय ते नमः ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namō'stvanantavīryāya mahāghōrāya tē namaḥ ।
namastē ghōraghōrāya viśvaghōrāya tē namaḥ ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the one of infinite prowess; salutation to the supremely terrible. Salutation to the terrible of the terrible; salutation to the universally terrible.
contentBlocks.meanings: namastu ananta-vīryāya — salutation to the one of infinite prowess; mahā-ghōrāya tē namaḥ — salutation to you, the supremely terrible; namastē ghōra-ghōrāya — salutation to the terrible of the terrible; viśva-ghōrāya tē namaḥ — salutation to you, the universally terrible.

contentBlocks.original:
नमः उग्राय शान्ताय भक्तेभ्यः शान्तिदायिने ।
गुरवे सर्वलोकानां नमः प्रणव रूपिणे ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namaḥ ugrāya śāntāya bhaktēbhyaḥ śāntidāyinē ।
guravē sarvalōkānāṃ namaḥ praṇava rūpiṇē ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the fierce one and also to the tranquil one; to the giver of peace to devotees. Salutation to the guru of all worlds; salutation to the one whose form is the Praṇava (Oṃ).
contentBlocks.meanings: namaḥ ugrāya śāntāya — salutation to the fierce one and the tranquil one; bhaktēbhyaḥ śānti-dāyinē — to the giver of peace to devotees; guravē sarva-lōkānāṃ — to the guru of all worlds; namaḥ praṇava-rūpiṇē — salutation to the one whose form is the Praṇava (Oṃ).

contentBlocks.original:
नमस्ते वाग्भवाख्याय दीर्घकामाय ते नमः ।
नमस्ते कामराजाय योषित्कामाय ते नमः ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namastē vāgbhavākhyāya dīrghakāmāya tē namaḥ ।
namastē kāmarājāya yōṣitkāmāya tē namaḥ ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the one called Vāgbhava (source of speech); salutation to the one of prolonged desire. Salutation to the king of desire; salutation to the one who fulfills the desires of women.
contentBlocks.meanings: namastē vāgbhava-ākhyāya — salutation to the one called Vāgbhava; dīrgha-kāmāya tē namaḥ — salutation to you, one of prolonged/great desire; namastē kāma-rājāya — salutation to the king of desire; yōṣit-kāmāya tē namaḥ — salutation to you, fulfiller of women's desires.

contentBlocks.original:
दीर्घमायास्वरूपाय महामायापते नमः ।
सृष्टिमायास्वरूपाय विसर्गाय सम्यायिने ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dīrghamāyāsvarūpāya mahāmāyāpatē namaḥ ।
sṛṣṭimāyāsvarūpāya visargāya samyāyinē ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: To the one whose form is the great Māyā; salutation to the lord of great Māyā. To the one whose form is the Māyā of creation; to the one who perfects emission (visarga).
contentBlocks.meanings: dīrgha-māyā-svarūpāya — to the one whose form is the great/long Māyā; mahā-māyā-patē namaḥ — salutation to the lord of great Māyā; sṛṣṭi-māyā-svarūpāya — to the one whose form is the creative Māyā; visargāya samyāyinē — to the one who perfects visarga (the creative emission/release).

contentBlocks.original:
रुद्रलोकेशपूज्याय ह्यापदुद्धारणाय च ।
नमोऽजामलबद्धाय सुवर्णाकर्षणाय ते ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rudralōkēśapūjyāya hyāpaduddhāraṇāya cha ।
namō'jāmalabaddhāya suvarṇākarṣaṇāya tē ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: To the one worshipped by the lords of Rudra's realm; and to the rescuer from calamities. Salutation to the one bound by the Ajāmalā [mantra]; salutation to the attractor of gold.
contentBlocks.meanings: rudra-lōka-īśa-pūjyāya — to the one worshipped by the lords of Rudra's realm; āpad-uddhāraṇāya cha — and to the rescuer from calamities; namaḥ ajāmala-baddhāya — salutation to the one bound by the Ajāmalā [mantra]; suvarṇa-ākarṣaṇāya tē — salutation to you, the attractor of gold.

contentBlocks.original:
नमो नमो भैरवाय महादारिद्र्यनाशिने ।
उन्मूलनकर्मठाय ह्यलक्ष्म्या सर्वदा नमः ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namō namō bhairavāya mahādāridryanāśinē ।
unmūlanakarmaṭhāya hyalakṣmyā sarvadā namaḥ ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutation, salutation to Bhairava, the destroyer of great poverty. To the one ever diligent in uprooting Alakṣmī (ill-fortune) — salutation always.
contentBlocks.meanings: namaḥ namaḥ bhairavāya — salutation, salutation to Bhairava; mahā-dāridrya-nāśinē — destroyer of great poverty; unmūlana-karmaṭhāya — to the one diligent/skilled in uprooting; hi alakṣmyāḥ sarvadā namaḥ — salutation always to the one who uproots Alakṣmī.

contentBlocks.original:
नमो लोकत्रयेशाय स्वानन्दनिहिताय ते ।
नमः श्रीबीजरूपाय सर्वकामप्रदायिने ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namō lōkatrayēśāya svānandanihitāya tē ।
namaḥ śrībījarūpāya sarvakāmapradāyinē ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the lord of the three worlds, to the one established in his own bliss. Salutation to the one whose form is the Śrī-bīja (Śrīṃ); to the granter of all desires.
contentBlocks.meanings: namaḥ lōka-traya-īśāya — salutation to the lord of the three worlds; sva-ānanda-nihitāya tē — to the one established in his own bliss; namaḥ śrī-bīja-rūpāya — salutation to the one whose form is the Śrī-bīja (Śrīṃ); sarva-kāma-pradāyinē — to the granter of all desires.

contentBlocks.original:
नमो महाभैरवाय श्रीरूपाय नमो नमः ।
धनाध्यक्ष नमस्तुभ्यं शरण्याय नमो नमः ॥ 17 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namō mahābhairavāya śrīrūpāya namō namaḥ ।
dhanādhyakṣa namastubhyaṃ śaraṇyāya namō namaḥ ॥ 17 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to Mahābhairava, to the one whose form is Śrī — salutation, salutation. O lord of wealth, salutation to you; to the one who grants refuge — salutation, salutation.
contentBlocks.meanings: namaḥ mahā-bhairavāya — salutation to Mahābhairava; śrī-rūpāya namaḥ namaḥ — salutation, salutation to the one whose form is Śrī; dhana-adhyakṣa namastubhyaṃ — O lord/superintendent of wealth, salutation to you; śaraṇyāya namaḥ namaḥ — salutation, salutation to the one who grants refuge.

contentBlocks.original:
नमः प्रसन्नरूपाय ह्यादिदेवाय ते नमः ।
नमस्ते मन्त्ररूपाय नमस्ते रत्नरूपिणे ॥ 18 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namaḥ prasannarūpāya hyādidēvāya tē namaḥ ।
namastē mantrarūpāya namastē ratnarūpiṇē ॥ 18 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the one of serene/pleased form; salutation to the primordial deity. Salutation to the one whose form is the mantra; salutation to the one whose form is the gem.
contentBlocks.meanings: namaḥ prasanna-rūpāya — salutation to the one of serene/pleased form; hi ādi-dēvāya tē namaḥ — salutation indeed to you, the primordial deity; namastē mantra-rūpāya — salutation to the one whose form is mantra; namastē ratna-rūpiṇē — salutation to the one whose form is a gem.

contentBlocks.original:
नमस्ते स्वर्णरूपाय सुवर्णाय नमो नमः ।
नमः सुवर्णवर्णाय महापुण्याय ते नमः ॥ 19 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namastē svarṇarūpāya suvarṇāya namō namaḥ ।
namaḥ suvarṇavarṇāya mahāpuṇyāya tē namaḥ ॥ 19 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the one whose form is gold; salutation again and again to the golden one. Salutation to the one of golden hue; salutation to the one of great merit.
contentBlocks.meanings: namastē svarṇa-rūpāya — salutation to the one whose form is gold; suvarṇāya namaḥ namaḥ — salutation, salutation to the golden one; namaḥ suvarṇa-varṇāya — salutation to the one of golden hue/color; mahā-puṇyāya tē namaḥ — salutation to you, the one of great merit/virtue.

contentBlocks.original:
नमः शुद्धाय बुद्धाय नमः संसारतारिणे ।
नमो देवाय गुह्याय प्रबलाय नमो नमः ॥ 20 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namaḥ śuddhāya buddhāya namaḥ saṃsāratāriṇē ।
namō dēvāya guhyāya prabalāya namō namaḥ ॥ 20 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the pure one, to the enlightened one; salutation to the one who ferries across saṃsāra. Salutation to the divine one, the secret one, the supremely powerful one — salutation, salutation.
contentBlocks.meanings: namaḥ śuddhāya buddhāya — salutation to the pure one and the enlightened one; namaḥ saṃsāra-tāriṇē — salutation to the one who ferries across saṃsāra; namaḥ dēvāya guhyāya — salutation to the divine one, the secret/hidden one; prabalāya namaḥ namaḥ — salutation, salutation to the supremely powerful one.

contentBlocks.original:
नमस्ते बलरूपाय परेषां बलनाशिने ।
नमस्ते स्वर्गसंस्थाय नमो भूर्लोकवासिने ॥ 21 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namastē balarūpāya parēṣāṃ balanāśinē ।
namastē svargasaṃsthāya namō bhūrlōkavāsinē ॥ 21 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the one whose form is strength; to the destroyer of others' strength. Salutation to the one dwelling in heaven; salutation to the one dwelling in the earthly realm.
contentBlocks.meanings: namastē bala-rūpāya — salutation to the one whose form is strength; parēṣāṃ bala-nāśinē — to the destroyer of [enemies'] strength; namastē svarga-saṃsthāya — salutation to the one established in heaven; namaḥ bhūr-lōka-vāsinē — salutation to the one dwelling in the earthly realm.

contentBlocks.original:
नमः पातालवासाय निराधाराय ते नमः ।
नमो नमः स्वतन्त्राय ह्यनन्ताय नमो नमः ॥ 22 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namaḥ pātāḻavāsāya nirādhārāya tē namaḥ ।
namō namaḥ svatantrāya hyanantāya namō namaḥ ॥ 22 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the one dwelling in the netherworlds; salutation to the supportless one. Salutation, salutation to the independent one; salutation, salutation to the infinite one.
contentBlocks.meanings: namaḥ pātāla-vāsāya — salutation to the one dwelling in the netherworlds (pātāla); nirādhārāya tē namaḥ — salutation to you, the supportless one; namaḥ namaḥ svatantrāya — salutation, salutation to the independent/self-willed one; hi anantāya namaḥ namaḥ — salutation, salutation indeed to the infinite one.

contentBlocks.original:
द्विभुजाय नमस्तुभ्यं भुजत्रयसुशोभिने ।
नमोऽणिमादिसिद्धाय स्वर्णहस्ताय ते नमः ॥ 23 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dvibhujāya namastubhyaṃ bhujatrayasuśōbhinē ।
namō'ṇimādisiddhāya svarṇahastāya tē namaḥ ॥ 23 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the two-armed one; salutation to the one resplendent with three arms. Salutation to the one accomplished in aṇimā and other siddhis; salutation to the golden-handed one.
contentBlocks.meanings: dvi-bhujāya namastubhyaṃ — salutation to the two-armed one; bhuja-traya-suśōbhinē — salutation to the one resplendent with three arms; namaḥ aṇimā-ādi-siddhāya — salutation to the one accomplished in aṇimā and other [siddhis]; svarṇa-hastāya tē namaḥ — salutation to you, the golden-handed one.

contentBlocks.original:
पूर्णचन्द्रप्रतीकाशवदनाम्भोजशोभिने ।
नमस्ते स्वर्णरूपाय स्वर्णालङ्कारशोभिने ॥ 24 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pūrṇachandrapratīkāśavadanāmbhōjaśōbhinē ।
namastē svarṇarūpāya svarṇālaṅkāraśōbhinē ॥ 24 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the one resplendent with a face-lotus radiant as the full moon. Salutation to the one whose form is gold; salutation to the one resplendent with golden ornaments.
contentBlocks.meanings: pūrṇa-chandra-pratīkāśa-vadana-ambhōja-śōbhinē — resplendent with a face-lotus radiating like the full moon; namastē svarṇa-rūpāya — salutation to the one whose form is gold; svarṇa-alaṅkāra-śōbhinē — resplendent with golden ornaments.

contentBlocks.original:
नमः स्वर्णाकर्षणाय स्वर्णाभाय च ते नमः ।
नमस्ते स्वर्णकण्ठाय स्वर्णालङ्कारधारिणे ॥ 25 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namaḥ svarṇākarṣaṇāya svarṇābhāya cha tē namaḥ ।
namastē svarṇakaṇṭhāya svarṇālaṅkāradhāriṇē ॥ 25 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the attractor of gold; salutation to the one who shines like gold. Salutation to the golden-throated; salutation to the wearer of golden ornaments.
contentBlocks.meanings: namaḥ svarṇa-ākarṣaṇāya — salutation to the attractor of gold; svarṇa-ābhāya cha tē namaḥ — salutation to you, the one shining like gold; namastē svarṇa-kaṇṭhāya — salutation to the golden-throated; svarṇa-alaṅkāra-dhāriṇē — to the wearer of golden ornaments.

contentBlocks.original:
स्वर्णसिंहासनस्थाय स्वर्णपादाय ते नमः ।
नमः स्वर्णाभपाराय स्वर्णकाञ्चीसुशोभिने ॥ 26 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
svarṇasiṃhāsanasthāya svarṇapādāya tē namaḥ ।
namaḥ svarṇābhapārāya svarṇakāñchīsuśōbhinē ॥ 26 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the one seated on a golden throne; salutation to the golden-footed. Salutation to the one beyond golden luster; salutation to the one resplendent with a golden girdle.
contentBlocks.meanings: svarṇa-siṃhāsana-sthāya — seated on a golden lion-throne; svarṇa-pādāya tē namaḥ — salutation to you, the golden-footed; namaḥ svarṇa-ābha-pārāya — salutation to the one beyond golden luster; svarṇa-kāñchī-suśōbhinē — resplendent with a golden girdle.

contentBlocks.original:
नमस्ते स्वर्णजङ्घाय भक्तकामदुघात्मने ।
नमस्ते स्वर्णभक्तानां कल्पवृक्षस्वरूपिणे ॥ 27 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namastē svarṇajaṅghāya bhaktakāmadughātmanē ।
namastē svarṇabhaktānāṃ kalpavṛkṣasvarūpiṇē ॥ 27 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the golden-shinned; salutation to the one whose very nature is the wish-cow (kāmadugha) for devotees. Salutation to the one who is the wish-tree (kalpavṛkṣa) for gold-seeking devotees.
contentBlocks.meanings: namastē svarṇa-jaṅghāya — salutation to the golden-shinned; bhakta-kāmadugha-ātmanē — whose very nature is the wish-cow for devotees; namastē svarṇa-bhaktānāṃ — salutation [to you] for gold-seeking devotees; kalpa-vṛkṣa-svarūpiṇē — to the one whose form is the wish-granting tree.

contentBlocks.original:
चिन्तामणिस्वरूपाय नमो ब्रह्मादिसेविने ।
कल्पद्रुमाधःसंस्थाय बहुस्वर्णप्रदायिने ॥ 28 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
chintāmaṇisvarūpāya namō brahmādisēvinē ।
kalpadrumādhaḥsaṃsthāya bahusvarṇapradāyinē ॥ 28 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the one whose form is the Chintāmaṇi (wish-gem); salutation to the one served by Brahmā and others. To the one dwelling beneath the wish-tree; to the giver of abundant gold.
contentBlocks.meanings: chintāmaṇi-svarūpāya — whose form is the Chintāmaṇi (divine wish-gem); namaḥ brahmā-ādi-sēvinē — salutation to the one served by Brahmā and others; kalpa-druma-adhaḥ-saṃsthāya — to the one dwelling beneath the wish-tree; bahu-svarṇa-pradāyinē — to the giver of abundant/many gold.

contentBlocks.original:
नमो हेमादिकर्षाय भैरवाय नमो नमः ।
स्तवेनानेन सन्तुष्टो भव लोकेशभैरव ॥ 29 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
namō hēmādikarṣāya bhairavāya namō namaḥ ।
stavēnānēna santuṣṭō bhava lōkēśabhairava ॥ 29 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Salutation to the attractor of gold and other [precious metals]; salutation, salutation to Bhairava. Be satisfied by this hymn of praise, O Bhairava, Lord of the World.
contentBlocks.meanings: namaḥ hēma-ādi-karṣāya — salutation to the attractor of gold and other [metals]; bhairavāya namaḥ namaḥ — salutation, salutation to Bhairava; stavēna anēna santuṣṭaḥ bhava — be satisfied/pleased by this hymn; lōkēśa bhairava — O Bhairava, lord of the world.

contentBlocks.original:
पश्य मां करुणाविष्ट शरणागतवत्सल ।
श्रीभैरव धनाध्यक्ष शरणं त्वां भजाम्यहम् ।
प्रसीद सकलान् कामान् प्रयच्छ मम सर्वदा ॥ 30 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
paśya māṃ karuṇāviṣṭa śaraṇāgatavatsala ।
śrībhairava dhanādhyakṣa śaraṇaṃ tvāṃ bhajāmyaham ।
prasīda sakalān kāmān prayaccha mama sarvadā ॥ 30 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Look upon me, O compassionate one, O beloved of those who seek refuge! O Śrī Bhairava, O lord of wealth, I take refuge in you — I worship you. Be gracious; bestow upon me all desires at all times.
contentBlocks.meanings: paśya māṃ — look upon me; karuṇā-āviṣṭa — O one pervaded by compassion; śaraṇāgata-vatsala — O one beloved to those who have taken refuge; śrī-bhairava — O Śrī Bhairava; dhana-adhyakṣa — O lord of wealth; śaraṇaṃ tvāṃ bhajāmi aham — I worship you as my refuge; prasīda — be gracious; sakalān kāmān prayaccha mama sarvadā — bestow all desires upon me at all times.
